tasks.label: Give written notice to your landlord or letting agent to end the occupation contract
tasks.dueText: At least 4 weeks' written notice under a standard occupation contract (Renting Homes (Wales) Act 2016)

tasks.label: Ask your landlord or letting agent about their checkout expectations
tasks.dueText: Checkout process, key return method, cleaning expectations, garden expectations, final inspection date & time

tasks.label: Book a removal company or hire a van
Set up Royal Mail post redirection (valid for 3–12 months)
Book end-of-contract professional cleaning if required by agreement (keep receipt)
Book professional carpet cleaning if required by agreement (keep receipt)
Book professional pet upholstery cleaning if required by agreement (keep receipt)
Notify gas, electricity and water/sewerage suppliers of your leaving date (water is usually Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water, or Hafren Dyfrdwy in parts of north-east / mid Wales)
Notify broadband provider: cancel, transfer, or take connectivity with you
Notify contents insurance: cancel, transfer, or update
Notify TV Licence of your move
Notify your local authority of your council tax leaving date and request a final bill
Give your landlord or letting agent a forwarding address for the deposit return and any final post
Cancel or return any resident parking permit(s) to the council
Replace dead lightbulbs with the same type/wattage as the inventory
Mow the lawn, weed and trim hedges if the garden is your responsibility
Repair minor damage permitted by agreement (filler, touch-up paint matched to existing)
Deep clean oven, hob, extractor fan and grill
Defrost and clean the fridge and freezer (allow 24 hours)
Clean washing machine drum, seal, detergent drawer and filter
Clean dishwasher filter and run an empty hot cycle with cleaner
Descale showerhead, taps, kettle and limescale on tiles
Bleach toilets including under the rim and behind the seat
Clean grout and silicone seals in bathrooms and kitchen if mouldy
Wipe skirting boards, door frames, light switches and sockets
Wipe inside all cupboards, wardrobes and drawers
Clean inside and outside of all windows and window tracks
Vacuum and mop every floor including under furniture and behind appliances
Remove all picture hooks and fill holes if the agreement requires
Empty and clean all bins; arrange large-item collection via the council or a charity
Take date-stamped photos and/or video of every empty room, cupboard and appliance
Clear all belongings from every room, plus the loft, shed, garage and any outbuildings
tasks.dueText: Ensure to check up high on shelves and cupboards

tasks.label: Photograph final meter readings (gas, electricity, heating, water)
tasks.dueText: Ensure the meter serial numbers are visible

tasks.label: Return all keys & fobs: front, back, windows, mailbox, communal, bin store, parking, garage
Switch off power at the consumer unit only if instructed
Conduct the check-out inventory inspection with your landlord or letting agent if possible; get it emailed or signed
Secure the property on your final exit (windows closed, doors locked, heating off, non-essential appliances off, lights off)
Submit final meter readings to suppliers the same day
Request deposit return in writing referencing the protection scheme (DPS, mydeposits or TDS)
tasks.dueText: Once you and the landlord agree the amount to be returned, it must be repaid within 10 days.

tasks.label: If there is a deposit dispute, raise it with the scheme's free adjudication service directly
Pay final utility bills (including water/sewerage) and only then cancel direct debits
tasks.dueText: Cancel direct debits only after final bills are paid, or you risk missed payments and credit problems.

tasks.label: Cancel the standing order for rent once the final payment has cleared
Confirm council tax account closed and final bill received
Update all banks, building societies, mortgage lenders and credit cards with your new address
Update ISA, investment, exchange and brokerage accounts with your new address
Update life insurance, critical illness and income protection with your new address
Update solicitor, accountant and will/executor records with your new address
Update your DVLA driving licence address (free; up to £1,000 fine if not done)
Update your DVLA V5C vehicle log book
Update your vehicle insurance address
Update your Blue Badge permit address
Update workplace and personal pension providers with your new address
Notify HMRC of your address change (Income Tax, National Insurance, tax credits, Child Benefit)
Update Companies House registered office and director's service address if running a business
Update the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) Update Service
Update Student Finance Wales address if a student
Update vet records and microchip database (Petlog/Identibase) if you have pets
Update Gift Aid declarations with charities with your new address
Update PayPal, Amazon, eBay and other frequently used online retailer default shipping addresses
Update gym and other subscription services (e.g. mobile provider) with your new address
Register on the electoral roll at your new address (for Senedd and local elections)
Check if any post is still arriving for you at the old address after 3 months
Keep your written statement of the occupation contract, inventory, photos and correspondence for at least 6 years
Reject deductions for fair wear and tear
tasks.dueText: Landlord must prove damage beyond normal use

tasks.label: Reject 'betterment' deductions
tasks.dueText: Landlord cannot charge new-for-old on used items -- replacement must be like-for-like

tasks.label: Reference the move-in inventory and your amendments for any disputes
tasks.dueText: Note any damage already present and documented at move-in. If the landlord's inventory images are poor quality, they may not prove the damage is new.

tasks.label: Direct deposit and end-of-contract correspondence to the party named on your written statement; check the landlord/agent on the Rent Smart Wales public register if unsure
